Change: tools/roll_agent_logs.py _ordered_sprint_keys now uses PREDECESSOR-TIME INHERITANCE with a RUNNING MAX -- undated sprints inherit the most-recent dated timestamp seen so far (insertion order), so trailing undated sprints stay newest (kept) and leading undated inherit epoch (archived); all-undated logs (every worker log) still early-return insertion order, so the zero-created_utc worker logs are now handled CORRECTLY without backfill. pm_bug_tickets.title: Labels and health bars missing from selectable non-prefab dynamic models
repro_steps: Observe heroes/enemies/workers/guards/tax collectors and prefab buildings
Check floating labels and health bars
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Dynamic selectable models have readable labels/health bars where appropriate; prefab building models do not regain permanent floating labels.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Labels/health bars are missing from models that should have them.
acceptance_test: Capture or screenshot evidence showing labels/health bars and no prefab building label clutter

pm_bug_tickets.title: Clicking directly on visible units often fails to select them
repro_steps: Click directly on visible heroes/enemies/workers several times
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Clicking directly on a visible unit selects it reliably.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Selection often fails even when the cursor is on the sprite/model.
acceptance_test: Manual or capture note confirming reliable unit selection

pm_bug_tickets.title: Holding G taxable gold overlay does not work
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Holding G shows taxable gold overlays; releasing G hides them.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Overlay is not working at all.
acceptance_test: Capture or screenshot evidence of overlay visible while G is held

pm_bug_tickets.title: Marketplace and blacksmith taxable gold display is grey 0
repro_steps: Open marketplace and blacksmith info panels after the economy has run
Inspect taxable gold value and styling
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Panels show accurate taxable gold values in readable styling, or truthful 0 when no tax exists.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Panels show grey 0 the whole time, suggesting stale UI or broken tax generation.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Heroes stand around idle much more than v1.5.6
repro_steps: Observe heroes after startup and around town
Compare behavior expectations to v1.5.6 active behavior
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Heroes seek useful activities instead of idling for long stretches.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Heroes still stand around idle a lot more; regression persists after R3.
acceptance_test: Agent notes describe root cause and comparison to v1.5.6 behavior

pm_bug_tickets.title: Guardhouse does not seem to have HP
repro_steps: Select or observe guardhouse under attack
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Guardhouse has HP, can be damaged/targeted, and exposes HP to selection/UI state.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Hero menu Chat button pins hero instead of opening chat
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Chat button opens the conversation popup without pinning the hero.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Chat button behaves like the Pin button.
acceptance_test: Manual/capture note confirming Chat opens chat and does not pin

round_meta.build_target: Fix three live gameplay failures: waves too weak (2x counts), heroes never eat at food stands, marketplace never gains gold from purchases. These all PASSED unit tests but FAIL in actual play.
round_meta.pm_note: IMPORTANT — Previous rounds wrote tests that pass but features do not work in live play. Every agent MUST verify their change works in a real headless sim run (observe_sync or direct SimEngine), not just mocked unit tests.

pm_status_summary.summary: R12: Three live gameplay failures from Jaimie R11 playtest. Root causes diagnosed by PM: (1) Wave compositions are too small (3-8 enemies); (2) Hunger checks skip heroes in FIGHTING state and heroes may lack gold; (3) Marketplace potions_researched=False by default so no hero ever buys potions, meaning potion-sale tax never fires. All had passing unit tests but fail in actual play.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Monster waves not impressive — counts too low
pm_bug_tickets.root_cause: _WAVE_TABLE in game/systems/wave_events.py has tiny compositions: Goblin Raid=4, Wolf Pack=5 total, Skeleton Patrol=5, etc. MAX_ALIVE_ENEMIES=48 is fine but the per-wave counts are low.
pm_bug_tickets.fix: Double all composition counts in _WAVE_TABLE. Raise MAX_ALIVE_ENEMIES from 48 to 80.
pm_bug_tickets.acceptance_test: Wolf Pack wave spawns at least 6 Wolf entities in headless test.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Heroes never eat at food stands in live play
pm_bug_tickets.root_cause: Three compounding issues: (a) tick_meal_hunger in basic_ai.py line 288-297 skips heroes in FIGHTING state, but with frequent waves heroes are almost always fighting. (b) should_seek_meal requires hero.gold >= FOOD_MEAL_COST_GOLD (10), but individual heroes may not have earned 10g yet from kills. (c) HUNGER_INTERVAL_MS=120000 (2 min) means first meal check is late.
pm_bug_tickets.fix_agent_05: Lower FOOD_MEAL_COST_GOLD to 5 in config.py. Lower HUNGER_INTERVAL_MS to 60000 (1 min). Ensure hero earns at least 5g from first kill (check ENEMY gold_reward values in config).
pm_bug_tickets.fix_agent_06: In ai/basic_ai.py, move the hunger check ABOVE the FIGHTING state check — or add a separate hunger check for IDLE heroes at the end of update_hero. In ai/behaviors/hunger.py should_seek_meal, lower _CRITICAL_HP_FRACTION from 0.25 to 0.15 so heroes eat while slightly damaged.
pm_bug_tickets.acceptance_test: In a 3-minute headless sim with food_stand + heroes, at least one hero calls buy_meal_at_food_stand.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Marketplace gold does not increase when heroes buy potions
pm_bug_tickets.root_cause: Marketplace.potions_researched=False by default (line 36 of economic.py). Potion research costs 100g player gold and takes time. No hero or system researches potions automatically, so heroes never buy potions, so potion-sale tax never fires. The R11 passive tax (100-400g/2min) should work but Jaimie may not have waited long enough, or the market.update() call path has an issue.
pm_bug_tickets.fix: Set potions_researched=True by default in Marketplace.__init__ (line 36 of economic.py). This makes potions available from minute 0. Also verify Marketplace.update() is being called by adding a print statement temporarily, running python main.py --no-llm for 3 minutes, and checking that stored_tax_gold increases.
pm_bug_tickets.acceptance_test: Marketplace.stored_tax_gold > 0 after 3 minutes of headless sim WITHOUT any hero purchasing anything (passive tax). Also > 0 if a hero buys a potion (sale tax).

pm_next_actions_by_agent.05: BUG-001 (wave counts 2x), BUG-002 (meal cost/timing), BUG-003 (potions_researched=True, verify passive tax in live sim).
pm_next_actions_by_agent.06: BUG-002 (hunger AI fires for fighting heroes, lower critical HP threshold).
pm_next_actions_by_agent.11: Run qa_smoke + verify live sim shows hunger/tax/wolf-pack working.

  This allows FIGHTING, IDLE, MOVING, SHOPPING, RESTING heroes to all be checked for hunger. The should_seek_meal function in hunger.py already gates on health_percent > critical threshold.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Double sim time advance per engine.update()
pm_bug_tickets.root_cause: GameEngine._prepare_sim_and_camera() (engine.py:1228) AND SimEngine.update() (sim_engine.py:605) both increment _sim_now_ms. Each update advances sim time by 2x dt.
pm_bug_tickets.fix: Remove sim time mutation from _prepare_sim_and_camera(). SimEngine.update() is the single authoritative sim time owner.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Sim time leaks through pause
pm_bug_tickets.root_cause: _prepare_sim_and_camera() advances _sim_now_ms at line 1228 BEFORE checking self.paused at line 1234. One advance leaks.
pm_bug_tickets.fix: Same fix as BUG-001: remove sim time mutation from _prepare_sim_and_camera(). With only SimEngine.update() advancing time, and sim.update() not called when paused, the leak disappears.

The fix is to REMOVE the sim time advancement from _prepare_sim_and_camera() in game/engine.py. SimEngine.update() should be the single authoritative sim time owner. Also ensure the advancement doesn't happen before the pause guard. After fixing, remove the xfail markers from tests/test_engine_sim_boundary.py tests 1 and 2 so they run as normal PASS tests.

TASK B - Consolidate Destroyed-Building Cleanup:
Read game/engine.py around line 1157 (_cleanup_destroyed_buildings / CleanupManager) and game/sim_engine.py around line 749. Agent 11 found that SimEngine does the real cleanup and GameEngine's path is redundant. If the GameEngine._cleanup_destroyed_buildings() call is dead/redundant, remove it. Keep the tests passing (they currently PASS, so don't break them).

TASK C - Delete Dead GameEngine Sim Helpers:
Search for call sites of these old helper methods in GameEngine:
  _update_ai_and_heroes, _apply_entity_separation, _update_world_systems,
  _update_peasants, _update_enemies, _process_combat, _process_bounties,
  _update_neutral_systems, _update_buildings
If a method has NO call sites (dead code), delete it. If it is still called by tests or tools, either move the test to SimEngine or add a tiny delegating wrapper with a docstring. Prefer deletion.

ONE TASK - FrameContext Design Or Minimal Implementation:

The performance audit found that get_game_state() and build_snapshot() are called multiple times per frame. Design or implement a FrameContext that is built once per frame and passed through render paths.

Read game/engine.py (the update/render methods) and game/engine_facades/render_coordinator.py to understand how many times snapshot and game_state are built per frame.

Minimum viable: Create game/presentation/frame_context.py with:
  @dataclass(slots=True)
  class FrameContext:
      snapshot: SimStateSnapshot
      game_state: dict

Then modify GameEngine to build FrameContext once and pass it where easy, reducing redundant snapshot/game_state construction. Do NOT change every caller in one pass — start with the main render path.

If the implementation is too risky for this sprint, just create the module with the dataclass and a TODO showing where it would plug in. A clean design doc section is an acceptable outcome.

  game/engine_facades/render_coordinator.py

TASK 1 — Define 5 Protocol interfaces in game/game_commands.py:
Replace the single GameCommands Protocol with:
  CameraCommands — camera_x, camera_y, zoom, zoom_by, center_on_castle

  MenuCommands — hud, pause_menu, debug_panel, dev_tools_panel, micro_view, audio_system, input_manager, show_perf, apply_hud_pin_action, capture_screenshot, send_player_message
  GameStateCommands — running, paused, display_mode, window_size, get_game_state, apply_display_settings, request_display_settings, try_hire_hero, place_bounty, process_command, plus the _private hooks (_skip_event_processing_frames, _borderless_drag_*, _last_ui_cursor_pos, _speed_before_pause, _perf_close_rect)

Below the Protocols, create EngineCommandHub that implements ALL 5 protocols by delegating to self._engine (a GameEngine). This replaces EngineBackedGameCommands.   self.selection: SelectionCommands = commands
  self.placement: PlacementCommands = commands
  self.menu: MenuCommands = commands
  self.state: GameStateCommands = commands
All 5 point to the same EngineCommandHub object. This is intentional.

Also fix the _engine abstraction leak: Find handle_keydown where InputHandler accesses c._engine directly for command mode (around lines 165-191). Replace with access through the GameStateCommands protocol (process_command is on GameStateCommands). You will need to read the actual command mode code carefully. The _command_mode and _command_buffer state need to be accessible through the protocol or through new properties/methods on EngineCommandHub.

TASK 4 — Update engine.py wiring:

Change: self.input_handler = InputHandler(EngineCommandHub(self))

TASK 5 — Backward compatibility + tests:

  GameCommands = GameStateCommands
  EngineBackedGameCommands = EngineCommandHub

wk66_close.pm_escape_hatch_decision: Move 4 (split SimStateSnapshot into RenderSnapshot + PresentationFrameState; L6) DEFERRED to WK67. Reason: Agent 10 Wave 2 left several consumers (worker/guard/tax draw, the Ursina unit/building loops, pygame bounty draw) reading live entities because the DTOs do not yet carry all their draw fields (anticipated by the plan -- keep live tuples for unmigrated consumers). So Move 4 clean snapshot split cannot remove those tuples, and the L6 extraction would require changing both renderers update() signatures for modest payoff. The keystone (L2) is already banked; Move 4 pairs naturally with WK67 get_game_state/AiGameView rework (same surface, Move 5) where the DTO migration can be finished and the live tuples actually removed. This is the planned PM escape hatch invoked at Gate 2.

wk67_kickoff.pm_status_summary: WK67 KICKED OFF 2026-05-29. Round A-2 = the AI boundary (Moves 5,6 + leak L9 + the cheap/L6 half of Move 4 + 3 determinism items), behavior-preserving (game plays byte-identical; the AI can no longer hold or mutate live sim services). Move 4(L6): split PresentationFrameState out of the sim snapshot (keep live entity tuples). Move 5: AiGameView + read-only WorldView; drop economy/sim/engine from the AI path; delete dead selected_* stubs. Move 6 (establish-pattern): shopping proposes HeroCommand, sim applies SYNCHRONOUSLY; builder-peasant via typed sim accessor. L9: new game/graphics/kenney_material.py, zero runtime tools imports in game/graphics. Determinism: anim frame->sim tick under DETERMINISTIC_SIM, _fog_revision stability, registered ursina_melee_combat scenario. Keystone guardrail = AI-decision digest byte-identical through Moves 5+6. DEFERRED to a render/Round-B sprint: the full Ursina-renderer DTO read-migration + deleting the live tuples (pairs with the ursina_renderer.py split). roll_agent_logs.py hardening EXCLUDED (separate agent per Jaimie).
pm_scope_decisions: FOCUS ON THE AI BOUNDARY (Jaimie 2026-05-29): WK67 = Move 5 + Move 6 + L9 + Move-4-presentation-split + 3 determinism items. DEFER the heavy Ursina-renderer DTO read-migration (deleting live entity tuples) to the render/Round-B sprint where ursina_renderer.py is split.
Move 4 here = PRESENTATION-SPLIT ONLY (L6). RenderSnapshot keeps BOTH live tuples AND WK66 DTO tuples; do NOT delete live tuples (Ursina still reads them). PresentationFrameState is engine-built.

Move 5 = AI CONSUMER PATH ONLY. AiGameView drops economy/sim/engine + wraps world in read-only WorldView; entity lists stay LIVE (AI-side DTOs deferred). UI get_game_state() dict untouched (UiGameView deferred to HUD split). Delete dead SimEngine.selected_* stubs.

Move 6 = ESTABLISH-PATTERN on the shopping write (economy.hero_purchase + hero.buy_item). Applier is sim-owned + SYNCHRONOUS (shopping reads hero.gold between purchases). Builder-peasant chop/harvest via typed sim accessor, NOT a command. Full command-ification deferred.
Move 6 ESCAPE HATCH at Gate 3: if a determinism/parity regression can't be resolved, bank Waves 0-2 + L9 + determinism (L3 read side already closed) and defer Move 6 -- PM call.
Determinism: ALL THREE items in WK67 (anim->tick, fog_revision, combat scenario).

Uncertainty to verify in W0/W2: whether BasicAI.update and the sim entity-tick share the same game_state dict; Move 5 scoped to the AI consumer path; sim-internal dict threading (hero.py/peasant.py world reads) is a Round-B follow-up.
